serviceName: Snowball Edge Compute Optimized with EC2 Compute Instances
serviceDescription: AWS Snowball, a part of the AWS Snow Family, is an edge computing, data migration, and edge storage device that comes in two options.

templates.title: Snowball Edge Compute Optimized with EC2 Compute Instances
templates.description: Edge computing applications, to collect data, process the data to gain immediate insight, and then transfer the data to AWS. Transfer data that is continuously generated by sensors or machines online to AWS from hospitals, factory floors, or at other edge locations.

components.content: Snow local compute and storage jobs supports Amazon S3 compatible storage on Snowball Edge Compute Optimized Devices. Amazon S3 compatible storage offers increased  scale, resiliency, expanded API, and features on single device or cluster setup. Amazon S3 compatible storage is charged based on usable S3 capacity provisioned.

cards.helpText: You can choose either monthly or 1 year upfront pricing. 3 year upfront pricing in no longer available for new customers.

components.label: Number of devices
components.placeholder: Enter the number of devices required

components.content: Shipping costs will vary based on your distance from the data center. Average shipping cost per device is $125.

Snow local compute and storage jobs supports Amazon S3 compatible storage on Snowball Edge Compute Optimized Devices. Amazon S3 compatible storage offers increased  scale, resiliency, expanded API, and features on single device or cluster setup. Amazon S3 compatible storage is charged based on usable S3 capacity provisioned.

Standard AWS service charges apply for usage incurred from importing or exporting data to or from your Amazon S3 buckets, such as Amazon S3 PUT requests or AWS KMS request charges (if KMS encryption is enabled on your Amazon S3 bucket).

components.content: Standard AWS service charges apply for usage incurred from importing or exporting data to or from your Amazon S3 buckets, such as Amazon S3 PUT requests or AWS KMS request charges (if KMS encryption is enabled on your Amazon S3 bucket).
